content:
maimai, released on July 11, 2012, is the debut entry in Sega's arcade rhythm game series, instantly recognizable for its distinctive circular touchscreen cabinet designed in collaboration with Sharp — a look that earned it the affectionate nickname "the washing machine." As the first version, it established the core gameplay loop that would define the series: notes radiate from the center of a round display and must be tapped, held, or traced on the touchscreen or eight surrounding buttons in time with the music. It introduced four foundational note types — Tap, Hold, Slide, and Break — along with a five-tier difficulty system on a 1–10 scale, where all Master charts were fixed at level 10. The game launched with three play modes: standard Single Play, head-to-head Versus Play, and cooperative Sync Play, with all multiplayer modes supporting up to four players across linked cabinets.

The scoring system centered around Achievement Rate, converting internal scores into a percentage from 0.00% to 100.00%, with All Perfect guaranteeing a perfect score. Players could save progress via Aime/BanaPassport NFC cards and track stats through the maimai.NET online service. A player level system awarded experience based on clear status, full combos, and sync performance, while level-gated unlocks encouraged progression. Each credit offered three tracks, with a bonus fourth track awarded for clearing all songs at 80% or above. Cabinet-mounted SD cameras supported gameplay recording with direct uploads to Niconico. Customizable settings included Guide Speed, Mirror Mode, Answer Sound, and Star Rotation indicators for slide velocity. Post-launch content was organized into an EXTEND folder — the only version to use this categorisation before subsequent releases folded additions into genre folders.

maimai PLUS, released on December 13, 2012, is the second version of the maimai arcade series and the first "PLUS" revision. It significantly refined the scoring system by introducing granular Break note judging — Break notes can now award 2500, 2550, or 2600 points depending on timing precision, which means Achievement Rate can exceed 100.00% with sufficiently precise play, giving rise to the concept of a "theoretical maximum." Two new ranks were added: AA (90% AR, sitting between S and A+) and SS (100% AR or above). The card system now remembers the last-played song and difficulty, so returning players no longer always start from the default selection. MASTER charts were also given a major rebalance — almost all returning songs had their MASTER difficulty re-rated, and MASTER charts could now appear at level 9 or 8 rather than being locked at level 10.

The game expanded its feature set with cross-level Versus Play, allowing players of different skill levels to compete together while each plays on their own chosen difficulty. A trophy system was introduced that tracked and displayed detailed achievement milestones across all songs. The track selection interface also began showing a degree-of-achievement display in the trophy list after play. Total song count grew to 106 tracks, with regular content updates continuing through the spring of 2013.

maimai GreeN, also known as 超, released on July 11, 2013, was the first full version upgrade for the maimai arcade series — a major overhaul rather than an incremental "Plus" update. The game's level-based progression system was replaced by a skill-based rating system (scored from 0 to 12, with two-decimal precision) derived from a player's best and recent performances. An in-game currency called maimile was introduced, earned through play and usable on collectible frames, nameplates, and maiChallenges. Two social systems debuted: the maiFriend system, which automatically registers players you play with and awards daily maimile bonuses based on friend activity, and maiNeighbourhood, which grants bonus maimile for playing after another player on the same cabinet. The difficulty scale was expanded from 10 to 12 levels, introducing the game's first level 11 and level 12 charts, and a new achievement rate system displayed play performance as a percentage — with scores above 100% achievable through frame-precise BREAK note timing.

The visual identity shifted to a light green-on-white color scheme, with the in-game UI adopting the ITC Bauhaus font for combo counts and achievement rates. The guide speed range was expanded from 1–6 to 1–9, and new mirror modes (vertical flip and 180° rotate) were added. Genre folders were reorganized: the ANIME/GAME combined category was split into separate folders, the J-POP label was renamed to POPS, and the OVERSEAS category was folded into POPS. The Touhou Project debuted as an entirely new genre — the first rhythm game to feature Touhou songs with accompanying character videos. Quality-of-life improvements included sortable and filterable song lists (by level, rank, version, genre, and recording eligibility), a two-handed Slide Skip gesture for quickly jumping between filter categories, and a toggle between Hit Songs and All Songs. Break note scoring was refined into frame-precise tiers, and sound effect options were adjusted so that turning off sound effects no longer silenced FULL COMBO and ALL PERFECT announcements.

maimai GreeN PLUS, released on February 26, 2014 and subtitled 檄 (geki, meaning "battle cry" or "manifesto"), saw a complete overhaul of the slide note system. Previously, slides were restricted to straight lines or simple circumferential arcs tracing the screen's edge. With this update, slides could now take on dramatically more varied curved shapes — including p, q, s, z, and v patterns — opening up far more creative and dynamic charting possibilities. Slide note judgment was also refined, with endpoint detection shifted slightly later to demand tighter precision. To complement this, the BREAK SOUND and SLIDE SOUND collections were introduced, giving players the ability to customize the audio feedback for break and slide notes. The genre folder structure received another rework: Touhou and niconico songs were separated into distinct folders, a new COLLABO genre was established for songs from collaboration events outside Sega, and select tracks were redistributed among the Variety, Game, and Original categories. Hold notes also received a subtle visual update — their endpoint markers changed from circular to hexagonal, making it easier to differentiate short staccato holds from ordinary tap notes at a glance.

Quality-of-life improvements included a revamped collection screen with faster switching between songs, icons, and titles, now accessible in any order. Rank announcements were enhanced so that grades A- and above now trigger spoken clear designations. The daily maiFriend maimile bonus display was consolidated to show collective totals from friends. A large-scale difficulty re-rating pass adjusted dozens of legacy charts, with many songs from the GAME and niconico categories seeing level bumps to better match their actual challenge. Two new ORIGINAL tracks were gated behind maimile purchases, establishing a model where premium original content could be unlocked through earned currency.

maimai ORANGE, released on September 18, 2014 and represented by the kanji 橙, is the third major upgrade to the maimai series, launching with 252 songs — a substantial expansion over its predecessor. The most significant new feature is the maiCHALLENGE mode, a purchasable challenge system where players spend maimile to activate time-limited objectives (such as achieving an All Perfect or Full Combo on a specific chart), with a strict 7-day window and a one-at-a-time limit. The version also introduces music tickets, a new in-game currency earned alongside maimile and used exclusively for unlocking songs, while maimile is now reserved for cosmetic Collections. The Guide Speed system was overhauled: the maximum numerical speed increased to 10, increments now adjust by 0.5, and a new Sonic speed setting was added above speed 10 for players seeking an extreme visual pace.

The visual theme shifts to a bright orange color scheme with the Shuriken Boy font defining the UI. A song recommendation engine was added, suggesting tracks based on a player's level and play history. The judgement system gained new judgement style options (Basic, Maji, Gachi, and Gori), each with progressively stricter timing windows. A Slide Skip option was introduced, and the version carried out a widespread difficulty rebalancing across Easy, Basic, Advanced, Expert, and Master charts for legacy songs. ORANGE also hosted the Tenkaichi Otogesai collaboration, cross-pollinating with other arcade rhythm titles through shared crossover tracks.

maimai ORANGE PLUS, released on March 19, 2015 and subtitled 暁 ("Dawn"), is the sixth version of the maimai series, building upon maimai ORANGE's framework. The most significant gameplay addition is the introduction of Re:MASTER difficulty — a tier above MASTER described as not merely harder but qualitatively different in chart design. Existing songs received Re:MASTER charts alongside the initial launch, and the difficulty continues to receive new additions through subsequent updates. The Home Ranker system was also introduced, posting a weekly top-three scoring leaderboard per arcade location with special presentation screens for ranked players. Quality-of-life improvements include a NEW GET category in the collection screen for quickly locating recently acquired items, track numbers now displayed as "Track 1/3" for clarity, and the option to hide one's Rating on results screens while the value is still tracked behind the scenes.

This version marks the first time songs were removed from the series — a total of thirteen early J-POP licensed tracks were retired. It is the final version to use maimile as the in-game currency until maimai でらっくす BUDDiES PLUS. System improvements include refined touch sensor detection, chart designer names displayed during song selection, scrolling long song titles, and a new start-screen animation. Collaboration events expanded significantly across this version's lifecycle, including tie-ins with Persona 4 Dancing All Night, CHUNITHM, and Sega Hard Girls.

maimai PiNK, released on December 9, 2015, introduced a major overhaul of the game's economy and user interface under the theme 桃. The existing dual-currency system of maimile and music tickets was consolidated into a single unified currency called MaiBerry, simplifying progression and song unlocks. The song selection flow was restructured to let players pick a category first, then a song, then a difficulty — with a new "Back" button to reselect. Difficulty levels were also refined: charts rated 7 through 11 received a "+" sub-designation for harder variants within the same level. The "mai CHALLENGE" mode was retired, with its dan plates made obtainable directly by achieving Rank S or above on all required songs. As a welcoming gesture, first-time players using a new Aime card were offered one free play.

Mechanically, PiNK expanded the note vocabulary with Break-attributed SLIDE stars and multi-endpoint SLIDE notes that fan out toward the finish, adding new layers of precision and dexterity to chart design. A new Challenge Track mode was added — a high-stakes gauntlet that forced a track to end early upon exceeding a threshold of Great-or-worse judgments, rewarding early song unlocks on successful clears. The ranking system was revised from the old D–SS scale to an F–SSS scale, introducing AAA and SSS tiers while removing the ± sub-grades on A and B. Visual and systemic housekeeping included a consolidation of song genre categories, the addition of an "Event Songs" category for time-limited collection events, and a large-scale purge of older name plates, frames, icons, and titles to reduce server load.

maimai PiNK PLUS, also referred to by the kanji 舞 (meaning "dance"), launched on June 30, 2016 as a mid-cycle upgrade to maimai PiNK. The visual theme continued the pink/warm aesthetic of the base version. The two headline additions were the fully revamped Dan Certification and the new Event Courses. The Dan Certification mode, accessible only on Track 1 with a RATING of 5.00 or higher, replaced the older challenge-based dan system with a proper course format: players must clear a set of 4 songs at a fixed difficulty, all achieving Rank S or higher, to earn a dan. A key risk-reward element was introduced from 9-dan onward — failing to achieve Rank S on any song immediately ends the run. Dan titles also gained color tiers reflecting higher performance, and previously earned dan nameplates were redesigned with a weathered "antique" appearance. The Event Course system similarly offered fixed 4-song sets on Track 1, but with a more accessible threshold of Rank A or higher on each song to earn exclusive collectibles.

On the system and quality-of-life front, Challenge Track life progression was made more generous, with HP increased every Monday and Thursday. Three previously MaiBerry-locked songs from PiNK became available by default. Judgment timing adjustment was added alongside optional FAST/LATE display indicators, allowing players to fine-tune sync and diagnose their accuracy. The Track Skip feature arrived in a post-launch update, letting players instantly terminate a stage if their score target became mathematically unreachable. Full Combo and All Perfect notification overlays were updated to no longer obscure the combo counter, achievement rate, and score. The achievement gauge received AA, AAA, S, and SS rank border marks, and a "minus achievement" display was introduced. A new EVENT song category was added, and the upper screen area gained a persistent credit counter display.

maimai MURASAKi, released on December 15, 2016, adopts a deep purple visual theme and introduces several major system changes, represented by the kanji 紫. The in-game currency was overhauled from MaiBerry to MaiZENNY, and the rating calculation method was revised. The Challenge Track LIFE progression now advances every 2 days with normal unlock after 14 days, and both MASTER and Re:MASTER charts unlock simultaneously upon clearing EXPERT with Rank S or higher. New difficulty levels were added — ADVANCED goes up to level 9 and MASTER up to level 13 — while note judgment notation reverted to all-uppercase.

The headline feature of this version is the Utagejou ("Banquet Hall"), a new song category inspired by CHUNITHM's WORLD'S END. It houses experimental charts built from scrapped or rejected concepts — featuring extreme simultaneous note clusters, variable-speed patterns, and unconventional layouts — accessible only to players with a maximum rating of 7.00+ who clear all preceding tracks at Rank S. Multiplayer also gained the FEVER system, where non-MISS judgments fill a FEVER gauge and a full-team FULL COMBO triggers MAX FEVER, while the 100% SYNC achievement now displays both the FULL COMBO (or ALL PERFECT) and SYNC indicators together on screen.

maimai MURASAKi PLUS, released on June 22, 2017 as the follow-up to the MURASAKi version and represented by the kanji 菫, brought a major overhaul to the Event Course system alongside several new gameplay features. The event progression model was restructured from a simple play-count system to a stamp card format, where players earn stamps by participating in event courses — with bonus stamps awarded for specific achievements such as clearing a difficulty for the first time. Collectibles and rewards are now gated behind stamp card completion rather than raw play counts, and each course was extended to run for six weeks instead of the previous four. Additionally, songs from expired event courses became automatically available to all players without requiring prior play.

The update also introduced a new event difficulty tier called Ultra Expert, which randomly selects four Master difficulty charts from across the entire song library with no genre or level restrictions. Another major addition was Otohime's Room, a side activity where players spend MaiZENNY to give presents to the character Otohime, building affinity over time to unlock special rewards. System-wide changes included the return of Re:MASTER charts after a brief absence, the removal of the home ranker feature, a collection random-set function for profile customization, and improved CHUNITHM cross-game linkage with near-instant data sync.

maimai MiLK, released on December 14, 2017, adopts a white-themed cabinet design and an anime/cafe visual aesthetic, represented by the kanji 白. The headline feature is the new Partner system: players choose one of three voiced companions — Ras, Chiffon, or Salt — before each play. Completing songs earns experience for the chosen partner, and leveling them up unlocks exclusive collection items. Their combined levels form the player's overall level, reviving a progression system absent since GreeN. The in-game currency was overhauled from maiZENNY to Cheese, earned as fragments per song and exchanged for collections, with a global ranking leaderboard tracking total Cheese collected.

The second major addition is the Survival Course mode, which replaces Event Courses with a life-based gauntlet of four tracks — hitting zero HP forces an early end, and the final track features an exclusive song unlockable through course completion. On the gameplay side, Challenge Tracks now allow Master charts to be played without first achieving Rank S on Expert, and all rating restrictions on the Dan course, Utagejou, and Challenge Track categories were removed. The Slide mechanic also received a refinement: when a song changes tempo mid-chart, slides now reference the new tempo for standby timing, fixing inconsistencies from previous versions. The maimaiNET portal was updated with a VS Friends score comparison feature, serial code redemption, and song filtering options.

maimai MiLK PLUS, released on June 21, 2018 and also known as 雪, is the PLUS update to MiLK, adding a winter/snow sub-theme over the existing white-and-dairy visual identity. The headline system addition is Present Events (プレゼントイベント): a linear milestone-based reward system where playing songs earns points that automatically unlock collection items at set thresholds — players cannot choose the next reward, similar to the stamp card system from MURASAKi PLUS. Bonuses are awarded for the first play of each day and for songs marked with a "Present Box +1" label. The ranking system also received a major overhaul: the old S/SS/SSS tiers were subdivided with the introduction of S+, SS+, and SSS+, which formally recognizes the theoretical maximum score — an All Perfect+ achieved when all Break notes score a critical 2,600 points. Additionally, Otohime, the mascot from maimai MURASAKi, was added as a new partner character.

The Survival Course mode was substantially reworked: each course now features four difficulty tiers, with songs 1–3 randomly drawn from the version's catalog and the fourth song in the highest tier set as a Re:MASTER chart — clearing that tier grants early unlock of the Re:MASTER chart before its regular release. On the system side, base Cheese earnings per song rose to 7 fragments (9 for new tracks), the character experience curve was rebalanced with new level thresholds, and characters now receive costume changes upon reaching every 100th level. The maimaiNET web portal gained the ability to filter the song list by version, and loading screen tips became more varied. This version also saw significant difficulty inflation, with four new charts pushing into the top tier of the original cabinet's rating system.

maimai FiNALE, released on December 13, 2018 and subtitled 輝, is the 13th and final version of the original cabinet series, serving as the grand finale before the series transitioned to second-generation DX cabinets. The visual theme emphasises radiance and closure, with a rich gold-and-white colour palette. Two new navigator partners, Shama and Milk, were introduced. On the system side, the Dojo grade-certification mode was rebranded as the Finale Course, which revamped the ranking format into regular grades, hidden "Secret" grades, and a Kaiden tier, with rating bonuses awarded upon clearing each stage. The Utage mode was also unlocked by default rather than requiring prior achievement thresholds.

Several new quality-of-life features debuted: Ras's Watching Diary, a daily login system that grants buffs including Rating Guard (preventing rating loss on weaker scores) and direct access to locked Master charts; a Track Skip option that auto-advances when a target rank becomes unattainable; and adjustable judgment timing with a Fast/Late indicator. The most prominent new gameplay mechanic was PANDORA BOXXX, a multi-stage unlock system that replaced Challenge Tracks, where players complete hidden conditions across six phases to unlock boss charts through an emblem-based progression system. New difficulty ceilings were introduced at Expert level 12+, Master level 13+, and Re:Master level 14.

maimai でらっくす, known by the kanji 熊 ("bear"), released on July 11, 2019, marks a complete overhaul of the series as the first version to run on the second-generation DX cabinet — 15% larger than its predecessor, with a brighter design, built-in 2.1-channel audio, a headphone jack, an expanded touch sensor array, and a still-photo memorial camera. It introduces a trio of new note types that fundamentally change chart design: Touch Notes (shutter-like markers requiring direct screen taps), Touch Hold Notes (held screen touches), and EX Notes (glowing notes that award Critical Perfect on all non-Miss judgments). Hold notes were also reworked to no longer require a deliberate release at the end. The scoring system was rebuilt from the ground up — achievement rates now have a theoretical maximum of 101.0000%, a new Critical Perfect judgment sits above Perfect, and the separate DX Score concept was introduced. Charts are now split into Standard Charts (pre-DX, no Touch or EX notes) and DX Charts (added from this version onward), switchable during play on a per-song basis.

System-level additions include Freedom Mode (a Japan-exclusive 10-minute free-play session for 2 credits), an online Friendly Match system for asynchronous data battles that raise a player's grade, and the physical Deluxe Pass card printed via Sega's Card Maker that grants perks like unconditional MASTER difficulty selection and extended Freedom Mode time. The progression model shifted to a map-based Area system where players advance through regions by equipping Tour Members, unlocking songs and characters along the way. The mascot でらっくま (Derakkuma) became the face of this generation. The menu UI switched to horizontal scrolling, and extensive visual customization options were added including selectable judgment line designs, note skins, star colors, slide display timing, and reaction effects. This version also established the nation-wide uniform pricing model and removed the EASY difficulty tier.

maimai でらっくす PLUS, subtitled 華 (Hana, "blossom") and released on January 23, 2020, introduced the return of the Partner system, last seen in maimai MiLK. Players select a partner from the cast of maimai FiNALE via a Stamp Card mechanic: one stamp is earned per day on the first play, and filling a 10-stamp card permanently unlocks that partner, who then replaces でらっくま on the result screen with unique voice lines. The Otomodachi Matching and Dan system were overhauled — when a player's grade is about to increase, a Boss Otomodachi appears with a fixed score representing a chart designer's record; victory promotes the player, while defeat incurs no demotion. Player grades of 2nd Dan or higher from DX were reset to 1st Dan.

The difficulty cap was raised from Level 14 to Level 15, triggering a comprehensive rebalance across most songs. Genre categories were restructured — VARIETY became GAME & VARIETY, while new "maimai" and "ongeki & CHUNITHM" categories absorbed songs from the disbanded Original folder. A wide range of new display options arrived: a 101% countdown achievement display, a decrementing DX Score display, FAST/LATE indicators for BREAK notes, a SYNC/VS display showing achievement rate differences against other players, a sensor visualization option revealing the touch panel's active zones, and partner-themed sound effects for BREAK and SLIDE notes. The result screen gained the DX STAR system, awarding one to five stars at 85% / 90% / 93% / 95% / 97% DX Score thresholds. The visual identity is centered on a floral, pink/magenta motif befitting the 華 theme.

maimai でらっくす Splash, known by the Chinese character 爽 ("refreshing"), released on September 17, 2020. It is the second major version of the maimai DX series and the 16th overall. The defining "shuwa-shuwa" (bubbly, soda-sparkling) theme permeates the visual identity and the new opening area, Shuwa Shuwa Chiho. Splash overhauled the Area unlock system, switching distance measurement from meters to kilometers and introducing Task Tracks — mandatory songs requiring an 80%+ clear to progress — and Perfect Challenges, life-bar-gated final boss songs at the end of an Area. All previous DX and DX+ Areas were retired, with their songs unlocked by default for all players. The Boss Otomodachi roster was refreshed, and the conduction song mechanic was introduced for the first time in the DX series, allowing qualifying players to share a special unlockable song with others. Tour member maximum levels rose to 9,999, a leader slot was added, and a Frames collection system debuted for full top-screen customization.

Other additions included new Break and Slide sound effects, a Splash-themed judgment line design, a damage sound volume setting, expanded timing adjustment with Offset B, the first song revival in maimai history, the introduction of Re:Master charts to the DX series, and a broad difficulty rebalance across existing tracks. The FULL SYNC DX achievement icon was also changed from "FDX" to "FSD."

maimai でらっくす Splash PLUS, also known as 煌, released on March 18, 2021, was the "Plus" upgrade to the Splash version. Its marquee feature was the Grade Certification (段位認定) Mode, a challenge mode that plays 4 predetermined songs back-to-back with a shared life gauge — survive to the end to pass the grade. A randomized variant, Random Grade Certification, was also added. The game introduced a new Mode Select screen where players choose between Normal Mode, Grade Certification Mode, and Freedom Mode, the latter forgoing area progression in favor of uninterrupted continuous play. A Ticket system was added, with consumable items purchasable with credits that boost area progress and assist gameplay; one free ticket is awarded daily upon clearing a Grade Certification. The Friend Match mode was completely overhauled with a class rank system spanning 26 tiers (B5 through LEGEND), where defeating opponents earns class points and climbing to LEGEND grants special rewards.

The visual theme kept the bubbly "Splash" aesthetic while updating DX Pass character art. On the systems side, the DX Pass structure was revamped: Bronze and Silver passes were removed, Platinum was eliminated, and Gold pass pricing was reduced. Area progression boost was decoupled from passes and moved entirely to the new ticket system. The rating calculation was restructured, removing Grade Certification rating from the total and retaining only New Song and BEST ratings, with a new Silver-White (白銀) rating color tier added. A new partner character, 乙姫 (Otohime), became unlockable via a daily stamp card. Quality-of-life additions included a settings menu accessible from the mode select screen to skip optional gameplay stages, and a result screen color swap so that new records display in dark blue. The previously Friend Match–exclusive conduction song was also made available to all players.

maimai DX UNiVERSE, released on September 16, 2021, is the third entry in the maimai でらっくす series, launching with a space-themed visual identity centered around a "Space Station" aesthetic and the kanji 宙. Its key new area, the permanent Space Station Area, introduced the Perfect Challenge mechanic requiring players to clear consecutive songs at high accuracy to unlock exclusive rewards. The Friend Matching mode received its first seasonal overhaul, incorporating season points and seasonal rankings with milestone and leaderboard rewards.

Quality-of-life improvements included the removal of the post-result "NEXT TRACK" screen to reduce downtime between songs, and a class system revision that lowered the starting rank for most players as classes were rebalanced for the new version. Stamp Cards were significantly expanded: they can now award DX Charts, icons, frames, and nameplates as rewards, and players gained the ability to switch stamp cards before completing them. Two new partner characters were introduced as stamp card unlocks alongside the universe theme. The version also continued cross-game integration with CHUNITHM and オンゲキ cabinets, allowing songs to be unlocked across arcades through linked play events. A new "induction song" mechanic was added, where certain tracks could be unlocked by reaching the LEGEND class or by playing alongside a LEGEND-class player.

# maimai DX UNiVERSE PLUS (星)

**Release date:** March 24, 2022

## Overview

**maimai DX UNiVERSE PLUS** is the append (PLUS) version of the UNiVERSE series, continuing the DX-era space and cosmic visual theme. The most notable new feature is **Invitation Play** ("お誘いプレイ"), which allows a solo player to open the unused side of the cabinet for free BASIC-difficulty play by another person once per day. If both players clear their charts (80% or above), the inviting player receives a bonus to area progress and a 1.5x area progress ticket. Additionally, playing in 2-player mode now grants an extra daily stamp on the Stamp Card. The Tour Member management system was also overhauled, re-introducing member locking so specific characters can be preserved during auto-set, and auto-set now supports ascending or descending level ordering.

The update brought significant system-wide changes: all dan (rank) certifications were reset with entirely new courses, Otomodachi classes were scaled down on carryover (e.g., LEGEND~SSS5 → SS5), and the "Space Station chiho 2" area opened with new challenge tasks and Perfect Challenge tracks. On the UI side, area progress, active tickets, and the current DX Pass design are now visible directly on the music select screen, a FULL SYNC status icon appears on the score graph during multi-player gameplay, and clearing a Perfect Challenge displays a dedicated rainbow-themed celebration screen. Numerous quality-of-life improvements were also added, including a gradual headphone volume fade-in on login, enhanced Tour Member level-up animations, and a sweeping difficulty rebalance affecting many EXPERT and BASIC charts.

maimai でらっくす FESTiVAL, released on September 15, 2022, is the fourth entry in the DX series and frame-named after a celebratory festival theme (祭). It debuts several significant chart-play innovations while expanding the song library past the 1,000-track milestone. The note system received the deepest overhaul since DX: EX Break notes (guaranteeing Critical Perfect on hit) and Break Holds/Break Slides appeared for the first time, turning sustained holds and slides into precision-dependent scoring notes. Slide paths were freed from prior constraints, allowing sharp multi-corner turns, curved edge-hugging trajectories, and overlapping path lines within a single slide. To help players navigate the enormous song catalogue, the version introduced a new song recommendation system with three categories alongside a returning Random Select feature that works within any filtered folder, plus a new Rank grouping option and a Quick Retry function in Freedom Mode.

The visual theme embraces a bright, festival-inspired palette with the Raz character serving as the system-voice narrator. Several quality-of-life system changes arrived with this version: the answer-sound volume scale was expanded from 1–5 to 1–10, and entirely new tap success sounds, break judgement sounds, and slide sounds were added as player-customizable options. Otomodachi Battle was made permanently available outside seasonal windows. Infinite Areas were unlocked by default, with all songs acting as area bonuses at a reduced 1 km bonus, and previously area-locked tour members became permanently selectable. The random dan system was refreshed, adding EXPERT 超上級, enabling Re:MASTER charts in MASTER-level draws, and introducing a new ultimate rank, 裏皆伝 (Ura Kaiden). The in-game resolution moved to 1920×1080 FHD, and an AP+ achievement counter was added.

maimai でらっくす FESTiVAL PLUS, released on March 23, 2023, is the mid-cycle upgrade to maimai DX FESTiVAL. This version introduced several significant quality-of-life and competitive features. A favorites system allows players to register up to 30 songs via the maimai DX NET website for quick in-game access under a dedicated category. A rival system was added, letting players compare scores with registered friends and toggle between achievement rate and DX Score displays. The chiho progression system gained a distance stocking mechanic: when blocked by a task track or perfect challenge, excess travel distance is saved (up to 99 km) and can be applied later — even carried over to a different chiho. Paid track-skip tickets were upgraded to also unlock MASTER and Re:MASTER charts immediately. Track skip options were expanded with life-based variants, and the pre-song splash screen now displays BPM, the chart designer name, and the player's current record. Invitation play was extended to support ADVANCED difficulty charts.

On the audiovisual and system side, the upper display gauge gained eight new configurable modes, including DX Score (subtractive type), judgment breakdown (separating Critical Perfects from regular Perfects), and a Fast/Late counter. Volume controls were refined: TOUCH notes and TOUCH HOLD notes received a dedicated touch judgment volume slider, while BREAK SLIDE notes got their own volume option. The cabinet's top LED now turns red on the credit's last track. All dan standings were reset, with an entirely new set of courses introduced. Cross-game unlock events with CHUNITHM and Ongeki allowed songs to be transferred between titles. Several collaboration and original chiho were cycled in and out, and previously "conduction-only" songs were made available to all players. The overall visual aesthetic continued the festival theme, with the version nicknamed "祝" (celebration) in the community.

maimai でらっくす BUDDiES, known by the Chinese character 双 ("pair" or "duo"), released on September 14, 2023. It is the fifth version of the maimai DX series and the 22nd overall. True to its name, BUDDiES places a heavy emphasis on cooperative multiplayer, visualized through a vibrant party-themed aesthetic. The key addition is the revival of the Utage Hall (宴会場, "Banquet Hall"), a category of novelty/party charts unlocked at 10,000+ DX Rating. Within it, newly introduced Buddy Charts (バディ譜面) are designed exclusively for two-player play on the same cabinet, featuring cross-hand patterns and physical interaction between players. These charts display a combined achievement rate (capped at 202%) and combo from both sides. Utage and Buddy chart results do not affect DX Rating or Total Score.

Beyond the multiplayer focus, BUDDiES brought several quality-of-life improvements: speed settings now adjust in 0.25-step increments from 1.00 to 10.00, songs can be sorted by BPM, tour member awakening animations can be skipped, and player names can now include hiragana and katakana characters (Japan only) with changes limited to once per 10 credits. The version introduced Sync Play as a guaranteed status when two players share a cabinet, with combined two-player rankings on maimai-NET. Area design evolved with the first-ever occurrence of multiple Perfect Challenges per Area, and a hidden sasakure.UK Area chain mechanic. Legend class promotion was locked until the season start, with Legend wins awarding a fixed 4 SP. All FESTiVAL Areas were retired with their songs unlocked by default.

maimai でらっくす BUDDiES PLUS (宴), released on March 21, 2024, is the mid-cycle upgrade to BUDDiES. This version brought back two long-absent features from the pre-DX era: the maimile currency returns for the first time since ORANGE PLUS, earned by completing weekly missions and spendable on items such as Weekly Area progress 2x tickets and Tour Member EXP 5x tickets, and the partner affection system returns after being absent since FiNALE, allowing players to give presents to partners to unlock partner-specific nameplates, frames, and icons. The favorites system was expanded — songs can now be favorited directly from the results screen, and collection items gain their own favorites and random select functionality.

On the competitive and system side, the internal rating threshold for X+ notation was lowered from X.7 to X.6, causing broad chart re-ratings. All dan standings were reset with an entirely new set of courses. The rating color-change animation now persists until skipped, and rival scores on the song select screen can be toggled by tapping the rival score area. The Party Area 2 progression map opened, and cross-game unlock events with CHUNITHM and Ongeki continued. Friend Match classes carried over from BUDDiES at reduced tiers. Several legacy original chiho were retired with their songs made permanently available, English notation was added across the UI, and new invitation appeal messages were introduced. The version's visual identity continues the party theme under the subtitle "宴" (banquet/feast).

maimai でらっくす PRiSM, released on September 12, 2024, is the 24th version in the maimai arcade series and the successor to maimai Deluxe BUDDiES PLUS. Also represented by the kanji 鏡 (meaning "mirror"), the version's visual identity is built around a blue color palette and prism/kaleidoscope motifs, reflected in its theme song and UI. Its flagship new feature is KALEIDXSCOPE Mode, an advanced survival-style unlock system where players discover hidden "Gates" and "Keys" across the in-game world, then challenge three-song courses on a shared life gauge to unlock exclusive hidden tracks. The mode employs a community-driven difficulty relaxation mechanic and culminates in a multi-phase narrative arc with exclusive boss songs, collectibles, and a new partner character.

Beyond KALEIDXSCOPE, PRiSM introduces a major overhaul to Area (ちほー) progression: distance is now aggregated across an entire credit rather than calculated per-track, the stock distance cap increases from 99 km to 999 km, and play bonuses need only be achieved once per credit to apply to all tracks. Quality-of-life improvements include lockable stamp cards, a "Random Select" option in the Favorites category, sorting songs by addition date, a new "PRiSM" judgment line design, condensed Tour Member awakening animations, and background colors that evolve with awakening count. Overall game tempo and transition animations were also sped up, and previously locked conduction songs and Area-exclusive tracks were unlocked for general play.

maimai でらっくす PRiSM PLUS, released on March 13, 2025, is the mid-cycle version upgrade to PRiSM, continuing the prismatic crystal aesthetic. Its kanji reading 彩 evokes a rainbow-like theme of color and brilliance, reflected in new content such as the "Nanairo Chiho" (Seven-Colored Area) for unlocking songs through progression and Perfect Challenge tracks. The visual presentation received attention with the addition of a Movie Brightness option, two new Judgment Display types (2-F and 4-E), and the introduction of "long" songs that consume two credits per play.

System-wise, the headline feature is Tour Member Super Awakening (超覚醒), allowing tour members to grow past level 9999 by gaining a permanent super awakening level and resetting to level 1 each time they surpass the cap, becoming "super good" in infinite areas with increased travel distance. Partner intimacy rewards have been expanded with a dedicated frame unlocking once a partner receives 1000 presents. A new Track Skip option automatically skips when the player's score falls below their highest registered rival's score. Perfect Challenge difficulty relaxation wait times have been significantly shortened, and Friend Match opponent selection now offers 5 choices per class tier. KALEIDXSCOPE progress and various tickets carry over from PRiSM, while maimile resets to zero and Dan ranking content is refreshed.

maimai でらっくす CiRCLE, released on September 18, 2025, introduces the headline Circle (サークル) team system, allowing players to form or join player-created circles (or be automatically placed into arcade-based default circles in Japan). Members earn circle points through gameplay — scaled by achievement rate, with bonuses for 2-player sessions with fellow circle members — and accumulated points unlock tiered rewards including maimile, tickets, partner presents, and collection items. A monthly leaderboard determines each user circle's class (ranging from White through Rainbow), displayed on profile cards as a metallic nameplate. The accompanying seasonal event, Circle Festa, pits circles against one another for exclusive time-limited rewards.

On the system side, MASTER and Re:MASTER charts from the original maimai でらっくす through BUDDiES are now unlocked by default, and all Stage 1 KALEIDXSCOPE courses are playable without prerequisites. The Rating calculation is adjusted: songs from the previous version count as new for rating purposes, and achieving ALL PERFECT(+) grants a bonus point. Grade Certification grades of 十段 and below are retained across versions rather than fully reset, and Area Progress 1.5x tickets are now awarded on every Grade Certification play. The Track Skip animation is significantly faster and auto-engages after a period of no input. Perfect Challenge condition relaxation times have been slowed to 3/10/17/24 days per step, and the 100-life period now requires clearing on EXPERT difficulty or higher. A new "CiRCLE" judgment line design option and a collection item preview feature have been added, while all maimile from the previous version is reset.

maimai DX CiRCLE PLUS, released on March 19, 2026, is a version upgrade to maimai DX CiRCLE. The marquee feature is Circle Challenge, a weekly cooperative mode where circle members collectively work toward an achievement rate goal on a shared challenge track to earn circle points and maimile. The DX Rating system has been expanded with star medal subdivisions starting at 14,000 Rating and a new rainbow "Kiwami" plate design at 16,000 and above, visually enriching the rating badge progression. Quality-of-life additions include the ability to view server-wide clear rate data directly in-game, long songs becoming playable in Freedom Mode for the first time, and favorite song registration from the preparation screen. A Chiho Boost mechanic also arrives, applying a progress multiplier to returning original chiho areas during limited-time revivals.

Several system-level changes accompany the launch. The recommended categories have been reworked: the "recommended based on your selection" category is removed entirely, and the "popular songs" category is restricted to players with a DX Rating below 4,000, with guests and new Aime users defaulting to it. The version category sort order is inverted to show newest versions first, and monthly circle point icon rewards are doubled. Rival score displays now show ALL PERFECT/FULL COMBO status. Master and Re:Master charts from BUDDiES PLUS are unlocked for all players from the start. Several chiho areas have been retired with their songs becoming permanently available, maimile balances are reset to 0 on launch, and dan rank carry-over has been adjusted so that players who cleared True Dan ranks in CiRCLE now start at 10th Dan in CiRCLE PLUS.
